channel.title: Stanford HAI News
channel.description: Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence news

item.title: Today's AI Talks Like “Nobody.” New Research Gives It Real Personality.
item.description: PsychAdapter lets researchers dial in on personality traits, age, and mental health characteristics to generate text that sounds like real individuals, opening the door to training simulations and personalized content.

item.title: Reading Today’s Headlines Through AI: A Real-Time Audit of Six Commercial Chatbots
item.description: In a new study, scholars measured how accurately popular AI chatbots answered questions about the emerging news and found substantial regional disparity, dependence on distinct information ecosystems, and acute fragility under imperfect prompts.

item.title: AI Coding Agents Fail at Teamwork
item.description: Two models working together perform worse than one alone, exposing a critical gap in artificial intelligence capabilities.

item.title: How AI is Transforming Scientific Discovery While Keeping Humans at the Center
item.description: From designing new antibodies to simulating 1,000 years of climate in a day, AI is transforming what's possible—but humans remain the ones deciding what matters.

item.title: AI Hiring Tools Can Yield Racial Bias and Systemic Rejection
item.description: The first large-scale study of hiring algorithms in the wild finds concerning patterns to how systems reject candidates.
